2606.10406.title: FOGO: Forgetting-aware Orthogonalization Optimizer
2606.10406.paper_summary: We argue that forgetting is not confined to continual learning but is a general optimization phenomenon: during standard training, dominant mini-batch gradients suppress rare but useful update directions, causing short-term forgetting at every step. When such knowledge is never revisited, these losses compound into long-term forgetting-the classical failure mode of continual learning. We introduce FOGO, a scalable optimizer that continuously detects and resolves gradient interference across both regimes. FOGO spectrally orthogonalizes momentum updates to prevent dominant directions from monopolizing optimization, then stores representative past directions in a compact codebook memory built on random projection, where pairwise distances are provably preserved in low-dimensional space. At each step, conflicts between the current update and stored directions are resolved via lightweight orthogonal correction and lifted back through a proximal step, with minimal overhead and no data storage. Across class-imbalanced classification, continual visual learning under domain and class shifts, continual fine-tuning of LLaVA-7B, and GPT-2 pretraining, FOGO consistently improves convergence and knowledge retention, outperforming Adam and Muon.
2606.10406.paper_summary_zh: 我們主張，遺忘並不僅限於持續學習，而是一種普遍的優化現象：在標準訓練過程中，主導的迷你批次梯度會壓制稀有但有用的更新方向，導致每一步的短期遺忘。當這些知識從未被重新訪問時，這些損失會累積成長期遺忘——這是持續學習的經典失敗模式。我們引入了FOGO，一種可擴展的優化器，能夠持續檢測和解決兩種情境下的梯度干擾。FOGO以光譜正交化動量更新，以防止主導方向壟斷優化，然後將代表性的過去方向存儲在基於隨機投影的緊湊代碼簿內存中，這裡的成對距離在低維空間中是可證明保留的。在每一步中，當前更新與存儲方向之間的衝突通過輕量級正交修正來解決，並通過近端步驟提升回去，這樣的開銷最小且不需要數據存儲。在類別不平衡分類、在領域和類別轉變下的持續視覺學習、LLaVA-7B的持續微調以及GPT-2的預訓練中，FOGO始終改善收斂和知識保留，超越了Adam和Muon。
2606.10406.author: Toan Nguyen et.al.
2606.10406.authors: Toan Nguyen, Yang Liu, Trung Le, Celso de Melo, Flora D. Salim
